CITYOF FAIRFIELD

Fair PoIiticaI Practices Commission
Conflict of Interest Division
428 J Street 
Suite 800
Sacramento, California 
95804-0807
re: Request for Advice
Dear Sir or Madam:
I am the City Attorney of the City of Fairfield and I am writing
this request for advice on behalf of Jay Bodutch, drl Assistant
Director of Planning and Development for the City of Fairfield.
I am familiar with FPPC Advice tetter No. I-89-575 (December
26,1989 to Don Vickers, Deputy County Counsel, Santa Barbara) in
which you state that membership in a country club is an asset
which may require disqsualification 
if 
the membership is
transferable for value. The purpose of this request for advice
is to determine whether the opinion in Advice Letter No. I-89-575
applies to the facts involving Mr. Bodutch.
1. 
Mr. Bodutch is a member of the Rancho SoIano Resort and
Racquet Club, a private, for profit club located in the City of
Fairfield. 
Mr. Bodutch paid a membership fee to join the club
and the membership is transferable for value.
2. The Rancho So1ano Resort and Racquet Club is located adjacent
to the City-owned Rancho Solano Golf Course. The CIub consists
of tennis courts, racquet baII courts, and exercise facilities.
Club members have access to the golf course on the same terms as
members of the public who are not club members.
3. 
In addition to the golf course and private club facilities,
there is 
a clubhouse building which houses a fine 
dining
restaurant, a bar and griII 
facility, 
a pro shop, and meeting
rooms. The clubhouse is owned by the City of Fairfield 
and
leased to the Rancho SoIano Resort and Racquet Club, a California
general partnership. 
The clubhouse facilities 
are open to all
members of the public.
4. 
A second City-owned golf course is proposed in Paradise
Valley, in the northeastern section of the City of Fairfield.
The Paradise Valley GoIf Course will not include a private club
or clubhouse facilities.


5. 
Mr. Boalutch's membership in the Rancho Solano Resort and
Racquet Club is "financial interest" as that term is used in
Government Code S87I03.
6. Mr. Bodutch may not make, participate in making, or use his
position to influence a clecision which wiII increase or decrease
the value of the memberships by at ]east $250.
7. 
As an Assistant Director of Planning and Development, Mr.
Bodutch has been assigned to coordinate the development of the
Paraclise vaIley Golf Course,
Is Mr. Bodutch prohibited from coordinating the development of
the Paradise VaIley GoIf Course because that project may have a
material financial effect on his Rancho Solano Resort anal Racquet
Club membership?
Thank you for your attention. 
Please feel free to calL me i.f you
have questions or need further information.
cerel
N/-;r,--L<-.)
Betsy
City
Jay Bodutch
BiII Daugherty
AUS S

sections.question: Is Mr. Bodutch prohibited from coordinating the development of the Paradise Valley Golf Course because that project may have a material financial effect on his Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club membership?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an Assistant Director of Planning must disqualify himself from coordinating development of a competing municipal golf course when he holds a transferable membership in a private club adjacent to an existing city golf course.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request to FPPC, not an FPPC response. References prior FPPC Advice Letter No. I-89-575. Contains numbered facts/statements (items 1-7) establishing the situation, followed by the specific legal question. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is Mr. Bodutch prohibited from coordinating the development of the Paradise Valley Golf Course because that project may have a material financial effect on his Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club membership?

embedding.summary: City Attorney of Fairfield requests FPPC advice on whether Assistant Director of Planning Jay Bodutch must disqualify himself from coordinating development of a proposed Paradise Valley Golf Course due to his financial interest in a membership at the adjacent Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club.
